articles.title: Fujifilm to launch analog instant camera 'instax mini 13'
articles.description: Fujifilm has announced the launch of “instax mini 13” (mini 13), a new entry model in the instax series of instant cameras, allowing users to enjoy on-the-spot photo printing. It will be available on April 24 in Japan. Alongside the mini 13, a range of matching accessories will be released simultaneously,…
articles.content:

articles.title: Iran closes Hormuz Strait again
articles.description: Iran has again closed the Strait of Hormuz, impacting global oil supply as President Trump warns against 'blackmail'. Read more at straitstimes.com.
articles.content:
TEHRAN - Iran’s military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again on April 18, prompting ships to abandon attempts to transit and President Donald Trump to warn Tehran against trying to “blackmail” the United States.

articles.title: Cunha steers Man Utd towards UCL at Chelsea's expense
articles.description: Manchester United took a giant step towards ending a two-year exile from the Champions League as Matheus Cunha scored the only goal in a 1-0 win at Chelsea on Saturday.
articles.content:

articles.title: Andersen shuts out Senators in Game 1 as Hurricanes take early series lead
articles.description: Frederik Andersen stopped all 22 shots he faced and the Carolina Hurricanes defeated the Ottawa Senators 2-0 in the opening game of the first round.

articles.title: Two water delivery contractors killed by Israeli forces in Gaza: UN
articles.description: "UNICEF is outraged by the killing of two drivers of trucks contracted by UNICEF to provide clean water to families in the Gaza Strip," it said in a statement.
articles.content:
JERUSALEM: Two civilian contractors delivering water for the UN children's fund were shot dead by Israeli troops in northern Gaza, UNICEF said on Saturday (Apr 18), expressing fury over the deaths the previous day.
